title: cortaderia quila
intro: Cortaderia quila, also known as the Queñua or Chuño plant, is a type of grass native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cortaderia quila include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory infections. Cortaderia quila can be prepared as a tea or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional Andean medicine to make a medicinal drink called "ch'uño," which is made from the plant's roots and leaves.
